title: Selecting a C++ Standard for a Package
text: Every C++ compiler default language standard, R pins one packages builds. default risen time. example first written C++98, opting C++11 interesting step. Modern R requires least C++11, made C++17 default R 4.3, since moved default C++20. mechanism choosing standard stayed throughout: set CXX_STD Makevars. package pins C++17 uses function C++ standard library’s <random> facilities, available since C++11.
code: . ├── DESCRIPTION                         # Package metadata ├── NAMESPACE                           # Function and dependency registration ├── R                                   # R functions │   ├── RcppExports.R                   # Autogenerated R to C++ bindings by Rcpp │   └── r11-package.r                   # Package documentation ├── README.md ├── man                                 # Package documentation │   ├── r11-package.Rd │   └── runif_cpp.Rd ├── r11.Rproj └── src                                 # Compiled code     ├── Makevars                        # Selects the C++ standard via CXX_STD     ├── Makevars.win     ├── RcppExports.cpp                 # Autogenerated R bindings     └── runif_cpp.cpp                   # C++ function using the <random> library

previous_headings: Selecting a C++ Standard for a Package
title: Selecting a C++ Standard for a Package
text: function src/runif_cpp.cpp draws n values uniform distribution using std::mt19937_64 std::uniform_real_distribution, <random> header. introduced C++11, package must compiled least standard. exported function also carries //' roxygen comments (trimmed excerpt ) Rcpp::compileAttributes() copies R/RcppExports.R generate help page.
code: #include <Rcpp.h> #include <random>  // [[Rcpp::export]] Rcpp::NumericVector runif_cpp(unsigned int n, double start = 0.0, double end = 1.0,                               unsigned int seed = 183) {   Rcpp::NumericVector u(n);    std::mt19937_64 engine(seed);                          // Mersenne Twister engine   std::uniform_real_distribution<double> u_dist(start, end);    for (unsigned int i = 0; i < n; i++) {     u[i] = u_dist(engine);   }    return u; }

previous_headings: Selecting a C++ Standard for a Package
title: Selecting a C++ Standard for a Package
text: src/Makevars, src/Makevars.win Windows, set CXX_STD variable R reads building package. Naming standard CXX11, CXX14, CXX17, CXX20 causes R pass matching flag compiler. Pinning specific standard CXX_STD makes package build way regardless host R’s default. current R, whose default C++20, CXX_STD = CXX17 selects C++17 explicitly, older R guarantees least standard. Raising CXX20 CXX23 request newer standard.
code: # Select the C++ standard the package compiles against. CXX_STD = CXX17

text: package can also request standard SystemRequirements field DESCRIPTION, example SystemRequirements: C++17. field-based approach alternative Makevars CXX_STD shown , package uses Makevars approach. Surfacing C++ code Rcpp requires Rcpp LinkingTo (headers used compile time) Imports (available run time).

code: # Generate one draw runif_cpp(1, start = 2, end = 20) #> [1] 16.56711  # Generate two draws runif_cpp(2, start = 5, end = 30) #> [1] 25.23210 15.99768
